content_en:
It [worldly life] is founded on distress yet you want it, Free of harm and distress

One should imagine that what befell him is multiplied, for this is when what he suffers will be easier on him. It is a habit of smart porters to put something heavy on top of what they are carrying, and then after taking a few steps, to remove the heavy object as

that makes what they are carrying feel lighter.

One should also wait, in times of prosperity for an attack of tribulation, so that if any tribulation befalls him, he should think of what remains instead of what he has lost so that when part of that befalls him it becomes very easy on him. Such as when someone loses some money so he counts what remains, and then considers the remaining as a profit. Or if someone imagines that he loses his eyesight, so that when he has ophthalmia, it becomes easy on him to bear this illness, likewise with the rest of harmful matters.

The prudent imagines in himself,

his tribulations before they befall him.

If they suddenly befall him, they do not surprise him because he had already imagined them. And the ignorant trusts the days, and he forgets the demise of those who came before him.

So when trials of times surprise him, with some trials he becomes lost.

Though if he was strict in his affairs,

patience would have taught him boldness

A member of the righteous predecessors said, “I saw a woman, whose youthfulness surprised me, I said, ‘(There is no doubt] This face has never suffered from sorrow ot sadness.’ She said, ‘Do not say that! For I do not know anyone who has suffered from what I have suffered. I had a husband who bought a sacrificial animal and slaughtered it, and we had two sons. The eldest told the one younger than him, ‘Come and I will show you how our father slaughtered the ewe.’ So he slaughtered his brother. When

Abverting Ghamm (Grief) and Hamm (Worry)

we started to look for him, he ran away and my husband died while looking for him.’ I said, ‘So how are you handling your sorrow?” She said, ‘If I could find aid in sorrow I would have employed it.’

Section One: Treating Sorrow, Sadness and Grief

Sorrow, sadness and grief may befall one due the prevalence of blackness, which should be treated with what will remove this blackness; which are the things that bring about joy. Grief freezes the blood while happiness heats it until its intrinsic heat is raised, both of them (happiness and grief) can harm, and may lead to death, if they are not immediately kept within moderate levels.

Averting Excessive Fear and Cautiousness of Death

to the future. A resolute person is he who prepares for

what he fears before it befalls him, and avoids excessive fear of what must inevitably befall him, because (in this case) his fear does not benefit him. Indeed the fear of Allah was so intensified in the hearts of many righteous people that they asked Allah to decrease it. The reason behind asking Allah such is that fear is like a lash; if a camel is lashed continuously it will worry, but the lash should be used when it is lazy in order to motivate it.

| YEAR AND CAUTIOUSNESS OCCUR in matters related

It was narrated that Sufyan al-Thawri told a young man who was sitting with him, “Do you want to fear Allah truly and rightfully?” The young man said, “Yes.” Sufyan said, “You are a fool! If you fear Him truly you would not be able to fulfill the obligatory acts!””'

" Sufyan al-Thawri: ibn Said ibn Masri, Aba ‘Abdullah al-Thawri, one of the great Imams and worshippers of this nation. He was titled ‘The Leader of Believers in Hadith’ and was well versed in safsir. He was the teacher of Abi Hanifah and Malik amongst others and died in the year 161H.

Averting Excessive Fear and Cautiousness of Death

Section One: Excessive Fear

A person of sound mind should not fear illness excessively, because it must inevitably befall him some time during his life and fearing what is inevitable is merely an increase in harm. As for fearing death and thinking about it, it is something that is hard to do but what eases it, is knowing that death is inevitable so caution does not benefit the person in anyway rather it increases one’s cautiousness. Every time a person imagines the intensity of death, he experiences it emotionally, this is why he should not picture death in his mind, he will only die once, not multiple times, and avoiding thinking about it will ease it.

A person should also know that Allah is able to make it easier, if He Wills, and that what is after death is more disturbing than death itself, because death is a bridge that takes us to our eternal dwelling. However, one should frequently think about death to work towards it, not just for the sake of imagination.

If the thought of leaving this life saddens the heart, then the cure is to know that this world is not a dwelling of satisfaction, rather its pleasure and satisfaction is in departing it, therefore this is not something that one should compete to possess. Thus, a wise person is saddened by leaving this life only because of the righteous deeds he will miss performing, and that is why the righteous predecessors used to be saddened by death as well. When Mu‘adh ibn Jabal was on his death bed, he said, “O Allah! You know that I did not love this world and living long in it for the beauty of flowing rivers or for planting trees. Rather for fasting hot days, forbearing in acts of worship for hours and joining scholars in circles of Your remembrance.”

Section Two: Curing the Whispers

When death befalls someone, he should know that it is an hour of serious suffering because it is a moment of severe pain, when he is leaving all objects of love, his loved ones, add to all that the horror of the throes of death and fear of where his wealth will go. Then Shayfan comes and attempts to have the slave [of Allah] become discontent with his Lord, he tells him, ‘Look at you! What made you die? Is it painful? You are leaving your wife, your children and you will be laid under the ground!’ So he might cause him to become discontent with his Lord, hating Allah’s decree, making him say things that include any kind of objection, or he might make him unjust in his will, giving some of the inheritors preference over others, so on and so forth. In this case, we need to cure the whispers of Shayfan and cure the self.

Abia Dawid reported from Abii al-Yusr (radiyAllabu ‘anhu) that the Prophet (8) used to say,

gl ue Sle! gle of ub Sef o

“T seek refuge in You that Shayfén flounders me at death.”?

2 Reported by Aba Dawid 1552-1553, al-Nasa’ [8:283] through the chain of ‘Afla‘ Mawla (emancipated slave) of Abi Ayyab from Abii al-Yusr that the Prophet (4) used to pray, “O Allah! I seek refuge in you from dying under abolishment, not being deliberate, I seek refuge in you from drowning, from burning and from senility, I seek refuge in you that Shayfan flounders me at death, I seek refuge in you that I die while escaping from fighting for Your sake and 1 seek refuge in you from dying because of a sting.”

Al-Khattab said, “His secking refuge in the ‘floundering of Shaysan at death’, that is if Shaytan seizes him when he leaves this world, preventing him from repenting or hinders him from mending his affairs, leaving an injustice or he makes him despair from the mercy of Allah or makes him hate death or be

Aberting Excessive Fear and Cautiousness of Death

In that minute J#ay/an tells his assistants, “If you miss him now [i-e. you do not succeed to lead him astray before he dies], you will never catch him again [i.e. you will never be able to deviate him at any other time].” As for the cure for these trials we should first mention that whoever is mindful of Allah while in good health, Allah will protect him when he is ill, and whoever observes Allah in his thoughts, Allah will protect him when he moves his bodily parts.

Ibn ‘Abbas (radiyAllahu ‘anb) narrated from the Prophet (48) that he said, “Be mindful of Allah and He will protect you. Be mindful of Allah and you will find Him before you. Know Him while in prosperity, He will know you in distress.””*

You have known the story of Prophet Yinus (Jonah) - when he had previous righteous deeds that enabled his release him from the trial he suffered. Allah, May He be exalted, said,

“Had he not been of them who glorify Allah, He would

sorry for this life, so he becomes discontented with Allah’s decree in dissolution, going to the hereafter, so one has a bad end and he meets his Lord while he is discontented with Him. It was narrated that the Shayfan is never tougher on a man as in death and he tells his assistants: ‘Take this person! if you miss him today you will never catch him again.’ We seck refuge in Allah from his evil, we ask him to bless our death and to have a good end.”

* Reported by al-Tirmidhi 2516, al-Tirmidhi said it is a good authentic hadith.

Also reported by Ahmad [1:307-308] and al-Bayhaqi in Shw‘ub al-imén and alAsma’ wa'l-Sifat p.16. See al-Durar al-Manthr (1:66] and Tafsir ibn-Kathir [7:91].

have indeed remained inside its belly (of the fish) till the Day of Resurrection.”

Due to the fact that Pharaoh had no righteous deeds, he did not find anything that he could attach to at the time of trial, and so it was said to him,

od TA tet eee ete oe “Now (you believed) while you refused to believe before and you were one of the evildoers and corrupters.”

‘Abdul Samad, the ascetic, said on his death bed, “O my Master! For this minute I have kept You [i.e. I preserved Your Mercy so You bestow it upon me at this moment]. Whoever was negligent when in good health will be neglected when sick.”

It was narrated that one of the companions saw an old man begging so he said, “This man was negligent regarding Allah’s orders when he was young, therefore Allah neglected him when he became old.”

The cure for this disease is to encourage the self, to tell it that it is simply a fraction of time that will pass and after it will come the complete rest as the Prophet (4%) said, “No anguish will befall your father after this day.”*
content_id:
Ia [kehidupan dunia] dibangun di atas kesusahan, namun engkau menginginkannya, bebas dari bahaya dan kesusahan

Seseorang hendaknya membayangkan bahwa apa yang menimpanya berlipat ganda, karena dengan demikian apa yang dideritanya akan terasa lebih ringan baginya. Sudah menjadi kebiasaan para pengangkut barang yang cerdas untuk meletakkan sesuatu yang berat di atas barang yang mereka pikul, kemudian setelah melangkah beberapa langkah, mereka menyingkirkan benda berat itu karena

hal itu membuat apa yang mereka pikul terasa lebih ringan.

Seseorang juga hendaknya menunggu di masa kemakmuran akan datangnya serangan ujian, sehingga jika ujian menimpanya, ia memikirkan apa yang tersisa alih-alih apa yang hilang, sehingga ketika sebagian dari ujian itu menimpanya, hal itu menjadi sangat ringan baginya. Seperti ketika seseorang kehilangan sejumlah uang, maka ia menghitung apa yang tersisa, lalu menganggap sisanya sebagai keuntungan. Atau jika seseorang membayangkan kehilangan penglihatannya, sehingga ketika ia terkena penyakit mata, menjadi ringan baginya untuk menanggung penyakit ini, demikian pula dengan perkara-perkara yang menyakitkan lainnya.

Orang yang bijaksana membayangkan dalam dirinya,

ujian-ujiannya sebelum menimpanya.

Jika ujian itu tiba-tiba menimpanya, ia tidak terkejut karena ia telah membayangkannya sebelumnya. Sedangkan orang yang bodoh mempercayai hari-hari, dan ia melupakan kematian orang-orang sebelumnya.

Maka ketika cobaan zaman mengejutkannya, dengan sebagian cobaan ia menjadi kebingungan.

Padahal jika ia tegas dalam urusannya,

kesabaran akan mengajarkannya keberanian.

Seorang dari generasi salaf berkata, "Aku melihat seorang wanita yang kecantikannya membuatku terkagum. Aku berkata, 'Tidak diragukan lagi, wajah ini belum pernah menderita kesedihan atau duka.' Ia berkata, 'Jangan berkata demikian! Karena aku tidak mengenal seorang pun yang menderita seperti apa yang aku derita. Aku memiliki suami yang membeli hewan kurban dan menyembelihnya, dan kami memiliki dua orang anak laki-laki. Yang sulung berkata kepada adiknya, 'Kemarilah, aku akan memperlihatkan kepadamu bagaimana ayah kita menyembelih domba.' Maka ia menyembelih saudaranya. Ketika

Menangkal Ghamm (Duka) dan Hamm (Kekhawatiran)

kami mulai mencarinya, ia melarikan diri dan suamiku meninggal saat mencarinya.' Aku berkata, 'Lalu bagaimana engkau mengatasi dukamu?' Ia berkata, 'Seandainya aku bisa menemukan pertolongan dalam kesedihan, niscaya aku telah menggunakannya.'"

Bagian Pertama: Mengobati Duka, Kesedihan, dan Kedukaan

Duka, kesedihan, dan kedukaan dapat menimpa seseorang karena dominannya kegelapan, yang harus diobati dengan apa yang akan menghilangkan kegelapan ini; yaitu hal-hal yang mendatangkan kegembiraan. Kedukaan membekukan darah sementara kegembiraan memanaskannya hingga panas alaminya meningkat, keduanya (kegembiraan dan kedukaan) dapat membahayakan, dan bisa menyebabkan kematian, jika tidak segera dijaga pada kadar yang wajar.

Menangkal Rasa Takut Berlebihan dan Kewaspadaan Berlebihan terhadap Kematian

terhadap masa depan. Orang yang bertekad kuat adalah yang mempersiapkan diri menghadapi apa yang ditakutinya sebelum hal itu menimpanya, dan menghindari rasa takut berlebihan terhadap apa yang pasti akan menimpanya, karena (dalam hal ini) rasa takutnya tidak memberi manfaat kepadanya. Sesungguhnya rasa takut kepada Allah begitu menguat di hati banyak orang saleh sehingga mereka memohon kepada Allah untuk menguranginya. Alasan di balik permohonan tersebut adalah karena rasa takut itu ibarat cambuk; jika seekor unta terus-menerus dicambuk, ia akan gelisah, tetapi cambuk harus digunakan ketika ia malas agar memotivasinya.

RASA TAKUT DAN KEWASPADAAN TERJADI dalam perkara-perkara yang berkaitan

Diriwayatkan bahwa Sufyan al-Thawri berkata kepada seorang pemuda yang duduk bersamanya, "Apakah engkau ingin takut kepada Allah dengan benar dan sepatutnya?" Pemuda itu berkata, "Ya." Sufyan berkata, "Engkau bodoh! Jika engkau benar-benar takut kepada-Nya, engkau tidak akan mampu menunaikan ibadah-ibadah wajib!"

Sufyan al-Thawri: ibn Said ibn Masruq, Abu 'Abdullah al-Thawri, salah seorang imam besar dan ahli ibadah umat ini. Beliau bergelar 'Pemimpin Kaum Mukminin dalam Hadith' dan sangat menguasai tafsir. Beliau adalah guru Abu Hanifah dan Malik di antara yang lainnya dan wafat pada tahun 161H.

Menangkal Rasa Takut Berlebihan dan Kewaspadaan Berlebihan terhadap Kematian

Bagian Pertama: Rasa Takut Berlebihan

Orang yang berakal sehat tidak sepatutnya takut berlebihan terhadap penyakit, karena penyakit pasti akan menimpanya pada suatu waktu dalam hidupnya dan takut terhadap apa yang pasti terjadi hanyalah menambah penderitaan. Adapun takut terhadap kematian dan memikirkannya, itu adalah sesuatu yang berat untuk dilakukan, tetapi yang meringankannya adalah mengetahui bahwa kematian itu pasti sehingga kewaspadaan tidak memberi manfaat apa pun kepada seseorang, bahkan justru menambah kekhawatirannya. Setiap kali seseorang membayangkan dahsyatnya kematian, ia mengalaminya secara emosional, itulah mengapa ia tidak seharusnya membayangkan kematian dalam pikirannya, ia hanya akan mati sekali, bukan berkali-kali, dan menghindari memikirkannya akan meringankannya.

Seseorang juga harus mengetahui bahwa Allah mampu memudahkannya, jika Dia berkehendak, dan bahwa apa yang terjadi setelah kematian lebih mengkhawatirkan daripada kematian itu sendiri, karena kematian adalah jembatan yang membawa kita ke tempat tinggal abadi kita. Namun, seseorang hendaknya sering memikirkan kematian untuk beramal menuju persiapannya, bukan sekadar membayangkan saja.

Jika pikiran meninggalkan kehidupan ini menyedihkan hati, maka obatnya adalah mengetahui bahwa dunia ini bukanlah tempat tinggal kepuasan, bahkan kesenangan dan kepuasannya justru terletak pada meninggalkannya, maka ini bukanlah sesuatu yang patut diperebutkan. Dengan demikian, orang bijaksana bersedih meninggalkan kehidupan ini hanya karena amal saleh yang akan terlewatkan, dan itulah mengapa para salaf juga bersedih menghadapi kematian. Ketika Mu'adh ibn Jabal berada di ranjang kematiannya, ia berkata, "Ya Allah! Engkau tahu bahwa aku tidak mencintai dunia ini dan hidup lama di dalamnya demi keindahan sungai-sungai yang mengalir atau demi menanam pohon. Melainkan demi berpuasa di hari-hari yang terik, bersabar dalam ibadah berjam-jam lamanya, dan bergabung dengan para ulama dalam majelis-majelis dhikr-Mu."

Bagian Kedua: Mengobati Bisikan-bisikan

Ketika kematian menimpa seseorang, ia harus mengetahui bahwa itu adalah saat penderitaan yang serius karena merupakan momen kesakitan yang hebat, ketika ia meninggalkan segala yang dicintai, orang-orang yang dikasihi, ditambah lagi kengerian sakaratul maut dan kekhawatiran ke mana hartanya akan pergi. Kemudian Syaitan datang dan berusaha membuat hamba Allah menjadi tidak ridha kepada Tuhannya, ia berkata kepadanya, 'Lihatlah dirimu! Apa yang membuatmu mati? Apakah itu menyakitkan? Engkau meninggalkan istrimu, anak-anakmu, dan engkau akan diletakkan di bawah tanah!' Maka bisa jadi ia menyebabkan orang itu tidak ridha kepada Tuhannya, membenci takdir Allah, membuatnya mengatakan hal-hal yang mengandung segala bentuk keberatan, atau bisa jadi ia membuatnya tidak adil dalam wasiatnya, mengutamakan sebagian ahli waris atas yang lain, dan seterusnya. Dalam hal ini, kita perlu mengobati bisikan-bisikan Syaitan dan mengobati jiwa.

Abu Dawud meriwayatkan dari Abu al-Yusr (radiyAllahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi (ﷺ) biasa berdoa,

gl ue Sle! gle of ub Sef o

"Aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari gangguan Syaitan saat kematian."

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud 1552-1553, al-Nasa'i [8:283] melalui sanad 'Afla' Mawla (budak yang dimerdekakan) dari Abu Ayyub dari Abu al-Yusr bahwa Nabi (ﷺ) biasa berdoa, "Ya Allah! Aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari mati dalam kehinaan, tidak dalam keadaan waspada, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari tenggelam, dari terbakar, dan dari pikun, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari gangguan Syaitan saat kematian, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari mati saat melarikan diri dari berperang di jalan-Mu, dan aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari mati karena sengatan."

Al-Khattabi berkata, "Berlindungnya beliau dari 'gangguan Syaitan saat kematian', yaitu jika Syaitan menguasainya saat ia meninggalkan dunia ini, menghalanginya dari bertaubat atau menghalanginya dari memperbaiki urusannya, meninggalkan suatu kezaliman, atau ia membuatnya berputus asa dari rahmat Allah atau membuatnya membenci kematian atau

Menangkal Rasa Takut Berlebihan dan Kewaspadaan Berlebihan terhadap Kematian

Pada saat itu Syaitan berkata kepada para pembantunya, "Jika kalian kehilangan dia sekarang [yaitu kalian tidak berhasil menyesatkannya sebelum ia mati], kalian tidak akan pernah menangkapnya lagi [yaitu kalian tidak akan pernah bisa menyesatkannya di waktu lain]." Adapun obat untuk ujian-ujian ini, pertama-tama kita harus menyebutkan bahwa barangsiapa menjaga Allah saat dalam keadaan sehat, Allah akan melindunginya saat ia sakit, dan barangsiapa mengawasi Allah dalam pikirannya, Allah akan melindunginya saat ia menggerakkan anggota tubuhnya.

Ibn 'Abbas (radiyAllahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan dari Nabi (ﷺ) bahwa beliau bersabda, "Jagalah Allah, niscaya Dia akan menjagamu. Jagalah Allah, niscaya engkau akan mendapati-Nya di hadapanmu. Kenalilah Dia di waktu lapang, niscaya Dia akan mengenalimu di waktu sempit."

Engkau telah mengetahui kisah Nabi Yunus (Jonah) — ketika ia memiliki amal saleh terdahulu yang memungkinkan pembebasannya dari ujian yang dideritanya. Allah, Maha Tinggi Dia, berfirman,

"Seandainya dia bukan termasuk orang-orang yang bertasbih kepada Allah, niscaya dia akan

menyesal terhadap kehidupan ini, sehingga ia tidak ridha dengan takdir Allah dalam kehancuran, menuju akhirat, sehingga ia berakhir buruk dan menemui Tuhannya dalam keadaan tidak ridha kepada-Nya. Diriwayatkan bahwa Syaitan tidak pernah lebih keras mengganggu seseorang selain saat kematian dan ia berkata kepada para pembantunya: 'Tangkap orang ini! Jika kalian kehilangannya hari ini, kalian tidak akan pernah menangkapnya lagi.' Kita berlindung kepada Allah dari kejahatannya, kita memohon kepada-Nya untuk memberkahi kematian kita dan memberikan akhir yang baik."

Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi 2516, al-Tirmidhi berkata hadith ini hasan sahih.

Juga diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad [1:307-308] dan al-Bayhaqi dalam Shu'ab al-Iman dan al-Asma' wa'l-Sifat hal. 16. Lihat al-Durar al-Manthur [1:66] dan Tafsir Ibn Kathir [7:91].

tetap tinggal di dalam perutnya (ikan) sampai Hari Kebangkitan."

Karena Fir'aun tidak memiliki amal saleh, ia tidak menemukan apa pun yang bisa ia pegang pada saat ujian, maka dikatakan kepadanya,

"Sekarang (baru engkau beriman) padahal sebelumnya engkau menolak beriman dan engkau termasuk orang-orang yang berbuat kerusakan."

'Abdul Samad, sang zahid, berkata di ranjang kematiannya, "Wahai Tuhanku! Untuk saat inilah aku telah menjaga-Mu [yaitu aku memelihara rahmat-Mu agar Engkau mencurahkannya kepadaku pada saat ini]. Barangsiapa lalai saat sehat, akan diabaikan saat sakit."

Diriwayatkan bahwa salah seorang sahabat melihat seorang lelaki tua mengemis, lalu ia berkata, "Orang ini telah lalai terhadap perintah-perintah Allah saat muda, maka Allah mengabaikannya saat ia menjadi tua."

Obat untuk penyakit ini adalah menyemangati jiwa, memberitahunya bahwa ini hanyalah sekejap waktu yang akan berlalu dan setelahnya akan datang ketenangan yang sempurna sebagaimana Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda, "Tidak ada lagi kesusahan yang akan menimpa ayahmu setelah hari ini."
content_ar: It [worldly الحياة] is founded on distress yet you want it, Free of harm and distress ينبغي للمرء imagine that what befell him is multiplied, for this is عندما what he suffers will be easier on him. It is a habit of smart porters to put something heavy on top of what they are carrying, and then بعد taking a few steps, to remove the heavy object as that makes what they are carrying feel lighter. ينبغي للمرء أيضاً wait, in times of prosperity for an attack of المحنة, لذا that إذا any المحنة befalls him, he should think of what remains instead of what he has lost لذا that عندما part of that befalls him it becomes very easy on him. مثل عندما someone loses some المال لذا he counts what remains, and then considers the remaining as a profit. Or إذا someone imagines that he loses his eyesight, لذا that عندما he has ophthalmia, it becomes easy on him to bear this المرض, likewise with the rest of harmful matters. The prudent imagines in himself, his tribulations قبل they befall him. إذا they suddenly befall him, they do not surprise him لأن he had already imagined them. And the ignorant trusts the days, and he forgets the demise of those who came قبل him. لذا عندما الابتلاءات of times surprise him, with some الابتلاءات he becomes lost. Though إذا he was strict in his affairs, الصبر would have taught him boldness A member of the الصالح predecessors said, “I saw a woman, whose youthfulness surprised me, I said, ‘(لا شك] This face has never suffered from sorrow ot الحزن.’ Sقال, ‘Do not say that! For I do not know anyone who has suffered from what I have suffered. I had a الزوج who bought a sacrificial animal and slaughtered it, and we had two sons. The eldest told the one younger than him, ‘Come and I will show you how our الأب slaughtered the ewe.’ لذا he slaughtered his الأخ. عندما Abverting Ghamm (الحزن) and Hamm (Worry) we started to look for him, he ran away and my الزوج died بينما looking for him.’ I said, ‘لذا how are you handling your sorrow?” Sقال, ‘إذا I could find aid in sorrow I would have employed it.’ الفصل One: Treating Sorrow, الحزن and الحزن Sorrow, الحزن and الحزن may befall one due the prevalence of blackness, which should be treated with what will remove this blackness; which are the things that bring about joy. الحزن freezes the blood بينما happiness heats it حتى its intrinsic heat is raised, both of them (happiness and الحزن) can harm, and may lead to الموت, إذا they are not immediately kept within moderate levels. Averting Excessive الخوف and Cautiousness of الموت to the future. A resolute person is he who prepares for what he fears قبل it befalls him, and avoids excessive الخوف of what must inevitably befall him, لأن (in this case) his الخوف does not benefit him. Indeed the الخوف of الله was لذا intensified in the القلوب of many الصالح people that they asked الله to decrease it. The العقل behind asking الله such is that الخوف is like a lash; إذا a camel is lashed continuously it will worry, but the lash should be used عندما it is lazy من أجل motivate it. | YEAR AND CAUTIOUSNESS OCCUR in matters related روي أنه Sufyan al-Thawri told a young man who was sitting with him, “Do you want to الخوف الله truly and rightfully?” The young man said, “Yes.” Sufyan said, “You are a fool! إذا you الخوف Him truly you would not be able to fulfill the obligatory acts!””' " Sufyan al-Thawri: ibn Said ibn Masri, Aba ‘Abdullah al-Thawri, one of the great Imams and worshippers of this nation. He was titled ‘The Leader of المؤمنون in الحديث’ and was well versed in safsir. He was the teacher of Abi Hanifah and مالك amongst others and died in the year 161H. Averting Excessive الخوف and Cautiousness of الموت الفصل One: Excessive الخوف A person of sound العقل should not الخوف المرض excessively, لأن it must inevitably befall him some time خلال his الحياة and fearing what is inevitable is merely an increase in harm. أما fearing الموت and thinking about it, it is something that is hard to do but what eases it, is knowing that الموت is inevitable لذا caution does not benefit the person in anyway rather it increases one’s cautiousness. Every time a person imagines the intensity of الموت, he experiences it emotionally, this is why he should not picture الموت in his العقل, he will only die حين, not multiple times, and avoiding thinking about it will اليسر it. A person should أيضاً know that الله is able to make it easier, إذا He Wills, and that what is بعد الموت is more disturbing than الموت itself, لأن الموت is a bridge that takes us to our eternal dwelling. غير أن, ينبغي للمرء frequently think about الموت to work towards it, not just for the sake of imagination. إذا the thought of leaving this الحياة saddens the القلب, then the الدواء is لمعرفة that this الدنيا is not a dwelling of satisfaction, rather its pleasure and satisfaction is in departing it, لذلك this is not something that ينبغي للمرء compete to possess. وهكذا, a wise person is saddened by leaving this الحياة only لأن of the الصالح deeds he will miss performing, and that is why the الصالح predecessors used to be saddened by الموت as well. عندما Mu‘adh ibn Jabal was on his الموت bed, قال, “O الله! You know that I did not المحبة this الدنيا and living long in it for the beauty of flowing rivers or for planting trees. Rather for الصيام hot days, forbearing in acts of worship for hours and joining العلماء in circles of Your الذكر.” الفصل Two: Curing the Whispers عندما الموت befalls someone, he should know that it is an hour of serious المعاناة لأن it is a moment of severe الألم, عندما he is leaving all objects of المحبة, his loved ones, add to all that the horror of the throes of الموت and الخوف of where his الثروة will go. Then Shayfan comes and attempts to have the slave [of الله] become discontent with his Lord, he tells him, ‘Look at you! What made you die? Is it painful? You are leaving your الزوجة, your الأولاد and you will be laid under the ground!’ لذا he might cause him to become discontent with his Lord, hating الله’s decree, making him say things that include any kind of objection, or he might make him unjust in his will, giving some of the inheritors preference over others, لذا on and لذا forth. In this case, we need to الدواء the whispers of Shayfan and الدواء the self. Abia Dawid reported from Abii al-Yusr (radiyAllabu ‘anhu) that النبي ﷺ (8) used to say, gl ue Sle! gle of ub Sef o “T seek refuge in You that Shayfén flounders me at الموت.”? 2 Reported by Aba Dawid 1552-1553, al-Nasa’ [8:283] through the chain of ‘Afla‘ Mawla (emancipated slave) of Abi Ayyab from Abii al-Yusr that النبي ﷺ (4) used to pray, “O الله! I seek refuge in you from dying under abolishment, not being deliberate, I seek refuge in you from drowning, from burning and from senility, I seek refuge in you that Shayfan flounders me at الموت, I seek refuge in you that I die بينما escaping from fighting for Your sake and 1 seek refuge in you from dying لأن of a sting.” Al-Khattab said, “His secking refuge in the ‘floundering of Shaysan at الموت’, that is إذا الشيطان seizes him عندما he leaves this الدنيا, preventing him from repenting or hinders him from mending his affairs, leaving an الظلم or he makes him despair from the الرحمة of الله or makes him الكراهية الموت or be Aberting Excessive الخوف and Cautiousness of الموت In that minute J#ay/an tells his assistants, “إذا you miss him now [i-e. you do not succeed to lead him astray قبل he dies], you will never catch him again [i.e. you will never be able to deviate him at any other time].” أما the الدواء for these الابتلاءات ينبغي لنا أولاً mention that whoever is mindful of الله بينما in الخير الصحة, الله will protect him عندما he is ill, and whoever observes الله in his thoughts, الله will protect him عندما he moves his bodily parts. Ibn ‘Abbas (radiyAllahu ‘anb) narrated from النبي ﷺ (48) that قال, “Be mindful of الله and He will protect you. Be mindful of الله and you will find Him قبل you. Know Him بينما in prosperity, He will know you in distress.””* You have known the story of النبي Yinus (Jonah) - عندما he had previous الصالح deeds that enabled his release him from the الابتلاء he suffered. الله, May He be exalted, said, “Had he not been of them who glorify الله, He would sorry for this الحياة, لذا he becomes discontented with الله’s decree in dissolution, going to الآخرة, لذا one has a bad end and he meets his Lord بينما he is discontented with Him. روي أنه the Shayfan is never tougher on a man as in الموت and he tells his assistants: ‘Take this person! إذا you miss him today you will never catch him again.’ We seck refuge in الله from his الشر, we ask him to bless our الموت and to have a الخير end.” * Reported by al-الترمذي 2516, al-الترمذي said it is a الخير authentic الحديث. أيضاً reported by أحمد [1:307-308] and al-Bayhaqi in Shw‘ub al-imén and alAsma’ wa'l-Sifat p.16. See al-Durar al-Manthr (1:66] and التفسير ibn-Kathir [7:91]. have indeed remained inside its belly (of the fish) till the Day of البعث.” بسبب the fact that Pharaoh had no الصالح deeds, he did not find anything that he could attach to at the time of الابتلاء, and لذا it was said to him, od TA tet eee ete oe “Now (you believed) بينما you refused to believe قبل and you were one of the evildoers and corrupters.” ‘Abdul Samad, the ascetic, said on his الموت bed, “O my Master! For this minute I have kept You [i.e. I preserved Your الرحمة لذا You bestow it upon me at this moment]. Whoever was negligent عندما in الخير الصحة will be neglected عندما sick.” روي أنه one of the الصحابة saw an old man begging لذا قال, “This man was negligent فيما يخص الله’s orders عندما he was young, لذلك الله neglected him عندما he became old.” The الدواء for this المرض is to encourage the self, to tell it that it is simply a fraction of time that will pass and بعد it will come the complete rest as النبي ﷺ (4%) said, “No anguish will befall your الأب بعد this day.”*
